perturbation.original_text: Recipient will not reproduce, use, disseminate, or disclose CI to any person or entity, except to its employees and authorized representatives (i.e., temporary employees, consultants, and contractors) who need to know the CI to further the Purpose and are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as restrictive as this NDA before having access to the CI.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient will not reproduce, use, disseminate, or disclose CI to any person or entity, except to its employees who need to know the CI to further the Purpose and are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as restrictive as this NDA before having access to the CI.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'authorized representatives (i.e., temporary employees, consultants, and contractors)' from the allowed recipients of CI, a contradiction is introduced. The initial statement allows for disclosure to consultants and contractors, whereas this modification limits it only to employees. This directly contradicts the previous allowance, creating uncertainty about permitted disclosures and leading to potential breaches. The contradiction affects section 2 itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidentiality Obligations . Recipient will not reproduce, use, disseminate, or disclose CI to any person or entity, except to its employees who need to know the CI to further the Purpose and are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as restrictive as this NDA before having access to the CI. Recipient is responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its representatives. Recipient will not modify, reverse engineer, create other works from, or disassemble any software programs contained in the CI without Discloser’s prior written cons ent. Recipient will treat all CI with at least the same degree of care as it treats its own information of similar sensitivity, but never with less than reasonable care. Recipient will stop use of and return or destroy all tangible CI promptly upon reques t, together with any copies, except as otherwise required by law. Recipient may disclose CI (A) as approved in a writing signed by Discloser or (B) as necessary to respond to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as required by law, or as ne cessary to establish the rights of either party, provided that Recipient promptly notifies Discloser upon receipt of the disclosure order and requests confidential treatment of any affected CI.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text by removing 'authorized representatives (i.e., temporary employees, consultants, and contractors)' from the list of entities to whom CI can be disclosed. This creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, which initially permitted disclosure to consultants and contractors under certain conditions. The modified text now limits disclosure solely to employees, thereby contradicting the earlier allowance and introducing an inconsistency. The Contradicted Text is nearly identical to the Changed Text, which shows it doesn't consider authorized representatives. Since the Change Text does not mention authorized representatives which would create a contradiction, and therefore it is a YES.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient is responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient is not responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its representatives.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'is responsible' to 'is not responsible' directly contradicts the established responsibility for breaches caused by representatives. This in-text contradiction makes it uncertain who is liable for breaches, which impacts enforcement of the confidentiality obligations. This contradiction impacts section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidentiality Obligations . Recipient will not reproduce, use, disseminate, or disclose CI to any person or entity, except to its employees who need to know the CI to further the Purpose and are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as restrictive as this NDA before having access to the CI. Recipient is not responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its representatives. Recipient will not modify, reverse engineer, create other works from, or disassemble any software programs contained in the CI without Discloser’s prior written cons ent. Recipient will treat all CI with at least the same degree of care as it treats its own information of similar sensitivity, but never with less than reasonable care. Recipient will stop use of and return or destroy all tangible CI promptly upon reques t, together with any copies, except as otherwise required by law. Recipient may disclose CI (A) as approved in a writing signed by Discloser or (B) as necessary to respond to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as required by law, or as ne cessary to establish the rights of either party, provided that Recipient promptly notifies Discloser upon receipt of the disclosure order and requests confidential treatment of any affected CI.
perturbation.justification: The original text states, 'Recipient is responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its representatives.' The changed text states, 'Recipient is not responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its representatives.' This directly contradicts the original statement and the contradicted text, which also states 'Recipient is not responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its representatives.', but before the change was contradictory. Since the changed text negates the recipient's responsibility, it creates a direct inconsistency, making it unclear who is liable. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient will treat all CI with at least the same degree of care as it treats its own information of similar sensitivity, but never with less than reasonable care.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient will treat all CI with the same degree of care as it deems appropriate.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the 'at least the same degree of care as it treats its own information of similar sensitivity, but never with less than reasonable care', this creates ambiguity. The change contradicts the specific level of care initially required, now allowing for subjective judgment. This weakens the enforcement of confidentiality and the original text, potentially leading to insufficient protection of CI. This affects section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidentiality Obligations . Recipient will not reproduce, use, disseminate, or disclose CI to any person or entity, except to its employees who need to know the CI to further the Purpose and are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as restrictive as this NDA before having access to the CI. Recipient is not responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its representatives. Recipient will not modify, reverse engineer, create other works from, or disassemble any software programs contained in the CI without Discloser’s prior written cons ent. Recipient will treat all CI with the same degree of care as it deems appropriate. Recipient will stop use of and return or destroy all tangible CI promptly upon reques t, together with any copies, except as otherwise required by law. Recipient may disclose CI (A) as approved in a writing signed by Discloser or (B) as necessary to respond to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as required by law, or as ne cessary to establish the rights of either party, provided that Recipient promptly notifies Discloser upon receipt of the disclosure order and requests confidential treatment of any affected CI.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the 'Confidentiality Obligations' section by stating 'Recipient will treat all CI with the same degree of care as it deems appropriate.' This directly contradicts the original text, which stipulated 'Recipient will treat all CI with at least the same degree of care as it treats its own information of similar sensitivity, but never with less than reasonable care.' The modified text introduces ambiguity and allows the recipient to determine the level of care, potentially leading to insufficient protection of CI, directly conflicting with the initial requirement of a specific level of care ('at least the same degree of care...but never with less than reasonable care'). This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient will stop use of and return or destroy all tangible CI promptly upon reques t, together with any copies, except as otherwise required by law.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient will stop use of all tangible CI upon request, but is not obligated to return or destroy it.
perturbation.explanation: By stating the Recipient is not obligated to return or destroy CI creates a direct contradiction. The obligation to return or destroy is a core component of protecting confidential information. The enforcement is affected due to the contradiction in section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidentiality Obligations . Recipient will not reproduce, use, disseminate, or disclose CI to any person or entity, except to its employees who need to know the CI to further the Purpose and are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as restrictive as this NDA before having access to the CI. Recipient is not responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its representatives. Recipient will not modify, reverse engineer, create other works from, or disassemble any software programs contained in the CI without Discloser’s prior written cons ent. Recipient will treat all CI with the same degree of care as it deems appropriate. Recipient will stop use of all tangible CI upon request, but is not obligated to return or destroy it. Recipient may disclose CI (A) as approved in a writing signed by Discloser or (B) as necessary to respond to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as required by law, or as ne cessary to establish the rights of either party, provided that Recipient promptly notifies Discloser upon receipt of the disclosure order and requests confidential treatment of any affected CI.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Recipient is 'not obligated to return or destroy' tangible CI, which directly contradicts the original obligation in the 'Confidentiality Obligations' section within the same document (the Contradicted Text) that they 'will stop use of all tangible CI upon request, but is not obligated to return or destroy it.' By removing the requirement to return or destroy, the changed text creates a direct conflict with the intended protection of confidential information outlined in the contradicted text. This falls under the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, because different sections of the contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Either party may terminate this NDA upon 10 days ’ written notice . However, all CI provided under this NDA will remain subject to the terms of this NDA.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may terminate this NDA immediately with written notice . However, upon termination, all confidentiality obligations are immediately waived.
perturbation.explanation: The initial clause allows termination with 10 days' notice while maintaining confidentiality obligations. This changed text now allows immediate termination and waives all confidentiality obligations upon termination. This is an in-text contradiction because the original text says the terms of the NDA will remain in place even after termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Termination . Either party may terminate this NDA immediately with written notice . However, upon termination, all confidentiality obligations are immediately waived. 5. General . All CI is provided “AS IS” and all warranties, express, implied, or otherwise are disclaimed. Any attempt to transfer all or part of a party’s rights or obligations under this NDA without the prior written consent of the other party will be null and void. This NDA may only be modified by mutual written agreement signed by authorized representatives of the parties. This NDA contains the complete agreement between the parties as to the subject matter, and replaces and supersedes any other communications, or agreements, whether oral or written concerning the subje ct matter. Regardless of conflict of laws principles, this NDA is governed by and construed in accordance with the substantive laws in force in the State of California. The parties will comply with all applicable export laws and regulations in their use and disclosure of CI. This NDA may be executed in counterparts and may be signed electronically or with handwritten signatures. The English lang uage version of this NDA will be the version used if any dispute arises.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed termination with 10 days' notice while maintaining confidentiality obligations. The changed text now allows immediate termination and waives all confidentiality obligations upon termination. This directly contradicts the statement that terms of the NDA will remain in place even after termination, as well as creates a conflict within the contradicted text (same location), making it a strong, clear, and impactful contradiction, based on the explanation given. This fits the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
